id: c_structuredContext_v3_ACCUMULATION_L40_Deciding to start a master’s degree or course
scriptId: cs_v3_ACCUMULATION_L40_Deciding to start a master’s degree or course
historyId: h_v3_ACCUMULATION_L40_Deciding to start a master’s degree or course

goal: Shift more into data science and machine learning rather than staying solely on backend work.
Build a portfolio of practical ML/data science projects with demonstrable results to show employers.
constraints: Employer education allowance covers only partial tuition; requires financing plan for remaining cost.
Need to minimize debt while covering the remaining cost.
Undergraduate degree in mechanical engineering; lacks advanced statistics and ML coursework; needs to build statistical foundations.
Relocation would be difficult for at least a couple of years; prioritize local or remote options.
Prefer GRE-free or GRE-optional admissions policies for graduate programs (no mandatory GRE).
decisions: Explore cost/benefit analysis between part-time master’s and stackable certificate paths for ML; draft business-case email to manager/HR for capstone using company data.
Develop a portfolio-focused project plan with milestones and deliverables.
open: Should I pursue a part-time master’s degree as a compromise between depth and continuity of income?
What financing options exist to cover the remaining tuition after employer allowance?
Evaluate pivot options to data science/ML (part-time master's vs certificate routes) for skill development and ROI.
How do evening/weekend/self-paced formats influence the choice between a part-time master’s vs certificates vs MOOCs?
Identify GRE-free or GRE-optional graduate programs in data science/ML that fit local/remote, part-time, and budget constraints.
